Enterprise has no formal impact focus at all; no KPIs, nor impact indicators. The enterprise has to build an IMM strategy and system from scratch. The TA support will focus on raising awareness on the value of IMM (insight in and demonstration of impact) and getting the  enterprises acquainted with concepts, tools and frameworks.

Enterprise has no formal impact focus at all; no KPIs, nor impact indicators. The enterprise has to build an IMM strategy and system from scratch. The TA support will focus on raising awareness on the value of IMM (insight in and demonstration of impact) and getting the enterprises acquainted with concepts, tools and frameworks.

FlexEAU is looking for various funding sources (of long-term financing) in order to bridge the public funding gap and to compensate for losses due to suspended investment in assets by the government. They are in conversations with various (impact) investors, hence any support will be useful. The enterprise is mainly looking for expansion of solarized boreholes (20 additional). Enterprise has several funding leads, but there is a big gap between realised vs. targeted fundraising. Relevant documents for fundraising (e.g. pitchdeck) are outdated and there is no clear and realistic fundraising strategy.

FlexEAU is looking for various funding sources (of long-term financing) in order to bridge the public  funding gap and to compensate for losses due to suspended investment in assets by the government.  They are in conversations with various (impact) investors, hence any support will be useful. The enterprise is mainly looking for expansion of solarized boreholes (20 additional).  Enterprise has several funding leads, but there is a big gap between realised vs. targeted fundraising. Relevant documents for fundraising (e.g. pitchdeck) are outdated and there is no clear and realistic fundraising strategy.
Enterprise needs and will benefit from follow-on (bonus) support to operationalise the IMM process (completed during the standard component of the programme).

Despite of their commercial background, Tier Data seem to be impact-driven since they explicitly seek to focus on an underserved market segment in the water sector: CBOs (youth and women-led). They would like to integrate an impact model in their current operations. They need to start from scratch.

Fill in the total number of people reached with your initiative at this exact time of measurement (beneficiaries, customers, users etc) with all of the Water, sanitation and IWRM activities. number of users (Aggregate nr of beneficiaries under drinking water, sanitation and IWRM and Capacity building)

Fill in total number of people currently reached with capacity building activities by the initiative, based upon documentation. Disaggregate Male/Female and disaggregate per type of event and provide nr of people per event.
Select current stage of your innovation/initiative
Provide total number of water facilities per type of facility (Nr of tap points per type) produced and provided by the initiative to users at time of measurement. Choose from list.

Number of litres water used and sold on average per day. (Calculated over a period of at least 1 month and preferably 3 months) Divide total volume by nr of days calculated. E.g if volume is measured, divide monthly volume by nr of days per month.

Total nr of individuals that have gained a position as a result of initiative at this time of measurement . Disaggregate by number of new jobs created and number of entrepreneurs/self-employed positions. Disaggregate by male/female.

\*\* Minimum requirement to the reporting indicator should follow National standards on daily wages and working conditions (Max 8 hours per day and max 6 days a week)

Total nr of beneficiaries disaggregated by Male/Female and Domestic/school/public users

Please also check the criteria per service level to see what level best matches your initiative. The initiative must fulfill all criteria to be considered at that service level.

1. Safely managed service:
2. the initiative provides an improved source of water (this includes: piped water, boreholes or tube wells, protected dug wells, protected springs, rainwater, packaged / bottled water, water that is delivered)
3. the initiative is located on the premises of the household or dwelling
4. the initiative provides drinking water whenever it is needed
5. the drinking water provided by the initiative has no presence of E.coli
6. the drinking water provided by the initiative contains no / limited levels of arsenic and fluoride (below WHO standards: < 0.01 mg/L arsenic and < 1.5mg/L fluoride
7. Basic service:
8. the initiative provides an improved source of water (this includes: piped water, boreholes or tube wells, protected dug wells, protected springs, rainwater, packaged / bottled water, water that is delivered)
9. the initiative provides water for users where the collection time is less than 30 minutes for a roundtrip including queuing
0. Limited service:

Drinking water from an improved source for which collection time exceeds 30 minutes for a roundtrip including queuing.

1. Unimproved service:

Drinking water from an unprotected dug well or unprotected spring

\* This is indicative of the reach and scale of the initiative in reaching new users with its products and services in terms of the service level that is being provided (as defined by JMP).

Fill in Number of individual customers that paid for the initiative's products or services. (AVERAGE PER MONTH)  calculated over past 3-12 months. 

\* Note to enumerator/pm: One customer usually provides one household with a product or service (Average 4/5 persons depending on demographics) Please take this along in analysis.

_Extra financial indicator for Via Water projects and start-ups under the response facility. _Calculate this by dividing the total annual income by total annual costs of the operation x 100. into a cost recovery percentage. Thus, answer the question, what percentage of annual costs are currently covered with income. Calculate this including staff costs and cost of depreciation, loans and savings for CAPEX.
23-01-2024: All SMEs had 2243 customers per month as baseline and at the end of the project 2658
19-12-2024: Not determined at this point. Will be done during impact verification
02-02-2024: Connection from toilet to Biodigester, where faeces are contained, treated and reused. The number is limited, since they are registered users/purchasers of the biodigester.
02-02-2024: Number of household toilets connected to biodigesters
01-07-2022: The Waste Water Treatment Pond was under renovation and hence no treatment of wastewater during the period
01-07-2022: This includes Truck Operators, Toilet Operators, Food Market Vendors, Compost Retailers
01-05-2020: Reported until December 2019 | 01-05-2020: Installation of Veronica buckets and other hand washing facilities. Reported up to December 2018.
Target for the PPP Innovation programme is leverage of at least 1:1.
28-01-2025: It is almost breakeven as per Year to date P&L numbers provided in Impact Report dated 15th July 2024.
Actual realized = 66 projects in total since 2014
Actual realized number of beneficiaries reached =  2.034.891 total from PPP Small + VIA Water from DGIS contribution
Realized leverage ratio up to 2018 for both VIA Water and PPP Small = 1.39

There were no leverage targets for VIA Water program. VIA Water established its own target of 4 million leverage:
